reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — headless-Chrome load+run inference harness + desktop+mobile+light/dark screenshots of all 5 pages

rubric.evidence: Load+run harness in headless Chrome (fresh profile, served copy) built the real feature-extraction pipeline (onnx-community/Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-ONNX, device:'wasm', dtype:'q8', pooling:'last_token', normalize) and embedded three sentences: dim=1024; cos('A cat sat on the warm windowsill' , 'A feline rested by the sunny window')=0.7110; cos(same , 'The stock market fell sharply on Tuesday')=0.2620. Related >> unrelated — real, non-degenerate vectors. Worker (worker.js) calls the real pipeline off the main thread.
rubric.notes: This is a genuinely distinct family (2025 LLM-backbone embedder, last-token pooling + query instruction), NOT a relabel of a built BERT embedder. Verified with the exact ONNX build, not a proxy.

rubric.evidence: Output is a 1024-d L2-normalizable vector per input. Main page renders the real pairwise cosine similarity matrix + a 2D PCA projection (variance explained). See-inside compares the SAME query embedded plain vs. wrapped as 'Instruct: {task}\nQuery:{query}' and reports cosine-to-answer vs cosine-to-distractor + the margin. Practical page ranks a corpus and re-ranks at Matryoshka dims (1024→32). Wild page does cross-lingual retrieval.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (unit vectors; cosine in [-1,1]) is correct and legibly surfaced; the instruction/last-token distinctives are made visible.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (onnx-community/Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-ONNX linked to card + base), declares-task (feature-extraction), declares-backend (WebAssembly/CPU), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance table lists model, task, architecture (Qwen3 decoder, last-token pooling), output 1024-d + MRL, pooling, query convention, params/dtype 'q8 (onnx/model_quantized.onnx)', backend 'WebAssembly (CPU) — no WebGPU required', ~585 MB, 100+ languages, Apache-2.0.
rubric.notes: Honest disclosure; backend/dtype/pooling match what worker.js loads (device:'wasm', dtype:'q8', pooling:'last_token').

rubric.evidence: Uses the shared createModelLoader (lib/model-loader.js). On a fresh profile the loader shows the honest absent state (Download ~585 MB) — no silent transfer; a valid current cached copy auto-inits with only a Clear-cached-model control. requiresWebGPU:false so it initialises on the CPU with no adapter gate.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract; per-model clear-cache + storage surfaced by the shared loader.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live=polite status regions on main + every use-case page; failures set a readable .err status ('Failed: <message>'). Empty-input guards ('Add at least two sentences', 'Fill in the query and both passages'). Loader surfaces checking→initialising→ready and Retry on failure.
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. Explicit partial/evicted and offline-cached cache-state drives were not separately exercised this pass (openQuestions).

rubric.evidence: Main: sentence-set chips, one-per-line textarea, Embed&compare, task/query/docA/docB inputs + 'Score plain vs instructed'. Basics: A/B textareas, example chips, Compare, plain-vs-instructed. Practical: query input + chips + corpus textarea + Search + 'Compare dimensions'. Wild: query input + chips + multilingual corpus + Search. Multi-model: two loaders + KB textarea + question chips + Retrieve&answer. 25 focusable controls; primary buttons/textarea >=44px, chips 36px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced via click; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run, result areas show muted placeholders and the matrix/projection are hidden; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile. Every number shown (cosines, PCA, ranked scores) is computed client-side from the on-device embeddings.
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; the PCA/cosine maths run on the real returned vectors.

rubric.evidence: conformance: buttons-have-names (all buttons carry text), images-have-alt (no content ; PCA is an inline SVG with role=img + aria-label). Similarity matrix uses row/col headers with title tooltips. Status regions role=status aria-live=polite; labelled inputs/textareas; chip groups have aria-label.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline; a screen-reader pass over the similarity matrix + PCA scatter would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix: scrollWidth-clientWidth==0 at 1280x800 AND 360x740 (DPR3, touch) on all 5 pages (main/basics/practical/wild/multi-model), 0 console errors, 0 failed requests. Matrix + PCA use overflow-x:auto scroll containers; grid-2 uses auto-fit minmax(300px,1fr); glance cells overflow-wrap:anywhere. Light+dark screenshots read against the design system.
rubric.notes: Support flipped to ok/ok on this driven pass.

rubric.evidence: All embedding runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the CPU-bound forward pass never blocks the main thread; results are posted back. The light client-side maths (cosine, PCA over a handful of vectors) is negligible on the main thread. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture).
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement while embedding a large batch on the CPU would confirm; batching many sentences is the main main-thread-adjacent cost and is bounded to <=12-20 inputs per run.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream/compute embeddings without long tasks INP
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread so it doesn't spike INP; yield if any main-thread loop could exceed ~50ms.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all embedding runs in models/qwen3-embedding-0-6b/worker.js; the main thread only does small cosine/PCA maths over <=20 already-computed vectors.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven pages stayed responsive; console clean (0 errors)

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep critical interaction work ahead of deferrable background work.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — embedding is dispatched to the worker on explicit user action (Embed/Search/Score); the UI thread stays free for input.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: off-thread dispatch in qwen3emb.js engine; no blocking calls on click handlers beyond posting to the worker

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and data tables without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap/gap/intrinsic sizing; wrap wide content (tables, SVG) in overflow-x:auto containers; set overflow-wrap on long unbreakable strings.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — similarity matrix + AB/MRL tables live in .matrix-scroll/overflow-x:auto; grid-2 auto-fit; glance cells + long ids use overflow-wrap:anywhere. Verified 0px overflow at 360x740 and 1280x800 on all 5 pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflow==0 on every page; screenshots read

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; label every control; give data tables proper header scope.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite status/loader regions; labelled inputs; similarity matrix uses th scope row/col; PCA SVG has role=img+aria-label.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); all pages link the shared stylesheet + crumb back to the catalogue.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring?
Screen-reader experience of the similarity matrix and PCA scatter — are the cosine values and cluster structure conveyed usefully?
Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete a cached file → Re-download; offline warm cache → still runs).
On a low-memory mobile device, does the ~585 MB q8 download + WASM runtime stay within budget? Should the page warn before download?
Quantify the instruction effect and MRL degradation across a labelled retrieval set (not just single examples) to show the average lift/loss.

followUpGoals.goal: Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for qwen3-embedding-0-6b across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR reading of the similarity matrix + PCA).
Add a labelled mini-benchmark: measure nDCG/recall lift from the query instruction and the accuracy retained at each Matryoshka dimension over a small fixed corpus.
Add a WebGPU fast-path toggle (fp16/q4f16) alongside the WASM default and compare embed latency on-device, with an honest adapter probe.

summary: qwen3-embedding-0-6b is a genuinely distinct, honest demo of a 2025 LLM-backbone retrieval embedder (Qwen3 0.6B decoder, last-token pooling, per-query instruction, 100+ languages, Matryoshka). Verified with the exact ONNX build in headless Chrome: real 1024-d vectors with related>>unrelated cosine (0.711 vs 0.262), off-main-thread inference, honest absent→Download / cached→auto-init loader states, and 0px horizontal overflow + 0 console errors at 360x740 and 1280x800 across all five pages (main + basics/practical/wild/multi-model RAG). Support recorded ok/ok. Remaining work: keyboard/SR traversal, explicit cache-state drives, and a labelled instruction/MRL benchmark. Revision 1.
